draft_requirements: Write enough practical detail for application developers to use the feature in a dali-ui app.
Ground natural-language behavior claims in the actual public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when the context pack is not enough.
Before writing examples, inspect the referenced public headers and relevant sample files when needed.
Prefer examples copied or adapted from existing samples when they match the feature; otherwise keep examples small and verify every API use.
After drafting, review the completed Markdown line by line against repository evidence and fix inaccurate explanations or code before returning.
repair_requirements: Repair only the problematic spans and the smallest surrounding context needed; do not rewrite the whole document.
For each listed issue, use the repository evidence and context pack to verify the corrected wording or code before returning.
If an issue reveals the same API misuse nearby, fix the nearby equivalent misuse too; do not make unrelated style rewrites.
After repair, re-check the edited lines against public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when needed.

rules: Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.
Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.
Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples.

usage_patterns.intent: Create and configure the primary object using public dali-ui APIs.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Prefer typed constructors and setters when available.

usage_patterns.intent: React to user or resource events through public signals.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Use signal connection APIs only with their owning trait or class.

rendered_prompt:
You are reviewing an existing DALi dali-ui application developer guide draft.
This is Phase 2.3.5: source-grounded prose review.

Primary goal:
- Improve natural-language accuracy and guide flow using repository evidence.
- Preserve the document's useful structure and examples whenever possible.
- Do not perform a full rewrite.
- Keep the revised user-facing guide in en only.
- Remove Korean, Japanese, Chinese, or other non-English prose from the revised guide.

Feature target:
- Feature: Scroll Bar (scroll-bar)
- Category: views-components
- Guide profile: dali-ui-app
- Audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

Review rules:
- Review every natural-language paragraph line by line against public headers, implementation files, samples, and UTCs when needed.
- Accuracy is the first priority. Flow is the second priority.
- Edit the smallest unit needed: word, sentence, paragraph, or section-local addition/removal.
- If a word-level change is enough, change only the word.
- If a sentence-level change is enough, change only the sentence.
- If an entire paragraph is based on a wrong premise, revise, add, or remove that paragraph.
- Preserve code blocks when possible.
- If prose can be corrected to match an already accurate code block, prefer changing prose.
- If the code block itself makes the prose inaccurate or breaks the guide flow, rewrite only the smallest necessary part of that code block and verify it against public headers/source.
- Do not invent APIs.
- Do not add generic filler just to make the document longer.
- Do not change section structure unless prose_review.allow_section_restructure is true or the current prose is clearly misleading.
- Prefer localized edits when prose_review.localized_edits_only is true.
- Preserve code blocks especially when prose_review.preserve_code_blocks is true.
- Prefer Dali::Ui::View, typed setters, fluent chaining, and view-tree idioms for app-facing guidance.
- Treat dali-core and dali-adaptor APIs as reference/context unless the feature explicitly requires them.

Original Markdown draft:
---
title: Scroll Bar
sidebar_label: Scroll Bar
category: views-components
---

# Scroll Bar

The Scroll Bar component provides visual feedback for scrollable content position and allows users to navigate through scrollable views.

## Table of Contents

- [Creating a Scroll Bar](#creating-a-scroll-bar)
- [Configuring Bar Appearance](#configuring-bar-appearance)
- [Controlling Visibility](#controlling-visibility)
- [Managing Scroll Position](#managing-scroll-position)
- [Using with ScrollView](#using-with-scrollview)

## Creating a Scroll Bar

Create a `ScrollBar` instance using the static `New()` method:

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/public-api/scroll-bar.h>

using namespace Dali::Ui;

ScrollBar scrollBar = ScrollBar::New();
```

The `ScrollBar` class inherits from `AbsoluteLayout`, so you can position and size it like any other view. To downcast a base handle to a `ScrollBar`, use `ScrollBar::DownCast()`:

```cpp
BaseHandle handle = scrollBar;
ScrollBar casted = ScrollBar::DownCast(handle);
if (casted)
{
  // Successfully downcast
}
```

## Configuring Bar Appearance

### Thickness and Color

Set the scroll bar thickness using `SetBarThickness()`:

```cpp
scrollBar.SetBarThickness(10.0f);
float thickness = scrollBar.GetBarThickness(); // Returns 10.0f
```

Set the scroll bar color using `SetBarColor()`:

```cpp
scrollBar.SetBarColor(Vector4(0.2f, 0.4f, 0.8f, 1.0f)); // RGBA
Vector4 color = scrollBar.GetBarColor();
```

### Corner Radius

Apply rounded corners to the scroll bar. You can set the same radius for all corners or specify individual values:

```cpp
// Same radius for all corners
scrollBar.SetBarCornerRadius(4.0f);

// Individual radius for each corner (topLeft, topRight, bottomRight, bottomLeft)
scrollBar.SetBarCornerRadius(Vector4(2.0f, 4.0f, 4.0f, 2.0f));

Vector4 radius = scrollBar.GetBarCornerRadius();
```

### Minimum Size and Offset

Set a minimum size to ensure the scroll bar remains visible even when content is large:

```cpp
scrollBar.SetBarMinSize(20.0f);
float minSize = scrollBar.GetBarMinSize();
```

Set the offset to create a gap between the bar and the container edges:

```cpp
scrollBar.SetBarOffset(5.0f);
float offset = scrollBar.GetBarOffset();
```

## Controlling Visibility

The `ScrollBarVisibility` enum controls when scroll bars appear:

- `ScrollBarVisibility::Auto` — Show only during scrolling
- `ScrollBarVisibility::Always` — Always visible
- `ScrollBarVisibility::Never` — Never visible

Control vertical and horizontal scroll bar visibility independently:

```cpp
scrollBar.SetVerticalScrollBarVisibility(ScrollBarVisibility::Auto);
scrollBar.SetHorizontalScrollBarVisibility(ScrollBarVisibility::Never);

ScrollBarVisibility vVisibility = scrollBar.GetVerticalScrollBarVisibility();
ScrollBarVisibility hVisibility = scrollBar.GetHorizontalScrollBarVisibility();
```

## Managing Scroll Position

### Setting and Getting Position

Set the scroll position programmatically:

```cpp
scrollBar.SetScrollPosition(Vector2(15.0f, 25.0f));
Vector2 position = scrollBar.GetScrollPosition();
```

### Updating Bar Size

When using `ScrollBar` standalone, call `UpdateBarSize()` to recalculate the bar proportions based on content and viewport dimensions:

```cpp
float scrollWidth = 100.0f;   // Total scrollable content width
float scrollHeight = 200.0f;  // Total scrollable content height
float viewportWidth = 50.0f;  // Visible viewport width
float viewportHeight = 100.0f; // Visible viewport height

scrollBar.UpdateBarSize(scrollWidth, scrollHeight, viewportWidth, viewportHeight);
```

### Updating Scroll Position

Update the scroll bar position to reflect content scrolling:

```cpp
scrollBar.UpdateScrollPosition(Vector2(10.0f, 20.0f));
```

### Hiding the Bar

Programmatically hide the scroll bar:

```cpp
scrollBar.HideBar();
```

## Using with ScrollView

`ScrollView` has built-in scroll bar support. Configure visibility directly on the `ScrollView` instance:

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/public-api/scroll-view.h>

ScrollView scrollView = ScrollView::New()
  .SetScrollDirection(ScrollDirection::Vertical)
  .SetVerticalScrollBarVisibility(ScrollBarVisibility::Auto)
  .SetHorizontalScrollBarVisibility(ScrollBarVisibility::Never);
```

When using `ScrollView`, the scroll bar is managed automatically and you do not need to create a separate `ScrollBar` instance or call `UpdateBarSize()` manually.
